article_title: Lab-Grown Leather Derived From T. Rex Collagen Debuts as a Luxury Handbag in Amsterdam, Drawing Paleontologist Pushback

content_preview.title: Lab-Grown Leather Derived From T. Rex Collagen Debuts as a Luxury Handbag in Amsterdam, Drawing Paleontologist Pushback
content_preview.summary: A collaboration between VML, The Organoid Company, and Lab-Grown Leather Ltd. has produced the first handbag from material engineered using reconstructed Tyrannosaurus rex collagen sequences, but paleontologists challenge the project's scientific framing.
content_preview.body_excerpt:
## Overview

A consortium of biotech and creative firms has unveiled what it calls the world's first product made from lab-grown "T. Rex Leather" — a luxury handbag displayed at Amsterdam's Art Zoo Museum beginning April 2, 2026. The one-of-a-kind piece, designed by avant-garde techwear label Enfin Levé, will be auctioned after a six-week exhibition with a reported starting price exceeding half a million dollars. While the companies behind the project frame it as a proof of concept for slaughter...

editor_notes.content_quality: Interesting synthetic biology piece balanced with scientific pushback.
editor_notes.source_verification: PR Newswire and Live Science — both on allowlist. Automated checks passed.
editor_notes.factual_accuracy: Good balance between company claims and paleontologist criticism.
